title: pseudopodospermum hispanicum subsp. hispanicum
intro: Pseudopodospermum hispanicum subsp. hispanicum, also known as Spanish silvery aster, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pseudopodospermum hispanicum subsp. hispanicum include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application and internal use.
